mechanism_narrative: A rising Federal Funds Rate, indicative of a monetary tightening cycle, increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and restricts credit availability, leading to negative pressure on S&P 500 valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has raised rates multiple times since mid-2004
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between FEDFUNDS level changes and 60-day forward returns remains positive for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a level considered to be its natural rate, it signals a tight labor market that could lead to wage-push inflation, prompting the central bank to tighten policy more aggressively, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently 5.2, having fallen steadily for two years
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when the UNRATE is below the trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: A factor representing real retail sales growth (e.g., YoY % change in RSAFS minus YoY % change in CPIAUCSL) shows a negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in industrial production reflects broad-based strength in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy, signaling higher corporate profitability and driving positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or negative relationship with 20-day forward returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls growth is a primary indicator of economic health, boosting investor confidence and expectations for future consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive short-term market reactions.
evidence_refs: fact:Market participants react strongly to payrolls data on release day
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly greater than the average return on non-trigger days.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the central bank is already in a tightening cycle, it forces policymakers to be more aggressive than anticipated. This joint condition of rising inflation and rising rates is particularly damaging for equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.8% AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Both inflation and policy rates are currently rising
failure_condition: A regression model including an interaction term for changes in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient for that term against 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts from high levels serves as a leading indicator of a slowdown in a critical sector of the economy, negatively impacting consumer wealth and spending, and thus predicting negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 12-month high
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market has been exceptionally strong, making it vulnerable to a reversal
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, indicating the signal is not reliable.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing quarterly real GDP growth is the most direct measure of a decelerating economy. This slowdown leads to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts, causing a decline in equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter annualized GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: The correlation between the signal (triggered instances of low growth) and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 5-year evaluation period.
